reasoning:
1. Baseline Value: 2026-03-01 is a Sunday, meaning the baseline anchor stems from the most recent known observation on 2026-02-28, which is 4.63%. This is a noticeable 8-bps leap from 2026-02-27 (4.55%), likely caused by end-of-month index rebalancing and accrued interest adjustments. This establishes a comparatively high bar to beat.
2. Market Trend: Based on search results, the 10-year Treasury yield dropped to 3.97% on February 27, hitting a 4-month low amid market prospects of the Federal Reserve implementing rate cuts. This downward general yield sequence puts downward pressure on the broadly correlating AAA corporate yields.
3. 2026-03-08 resolution: Because 03-08 is a Sunday, it will refer back to Friday 03-06. Over just one week, the probability of overcoming the existing downward momentum to clear the high 4.63% baseline is relatively low. (0.35)
4. 2026-03-31 resolution: The one-month horizon means broader variance. Macroeconomic data releases (like NFP or CPI) and broader market changes have time to materialize and potentially shift yields upwards, drawing the certainty nearer to a coin-flip. Nevertheless, the recent downward momentum in Treasury yields maintains a slight lean against an increase. (0.42)
